title: El Gran Test Adaptativo
description: Evaluación integral que cubre gramática, vocabulario, lectura y pensamiento crítico.

questions.question: [A2 - Grammar] I ___ from Mexico. (am/is/are)
questions.explanation: I → am. First person singular of "to be".

questions.question: [B1 - Grammar] If I had more time, I ___ learn Japanese.
questions.explanation: Second conditional: If + past simple, would + infinitive. Hypothetical present/future.

questions.question: [B1 - Grammar] She wishes she ___ speak French. (could/can)
questions.explanation: Wish + past tense (could) for unreal present desires.

questions.question: [B2 - Grammar] Had I known earlier, I ___ have prepared better.
questions.explanation: Third conditional inverted: Had I known = If I had known. Would have + past participle.

questions.question: [B2 - Reading] The author's use of "allegedly" suggests that:
options: The author doubts or hasn't verified the claim
It's a direct quote from someone
questions.explanation: "Allegedly" signals unverified information — the author maintains distance from the claim.

questions.question: [B1 - Vocabulary] "I'm looking forward to the meeting." What does "looking forward to" mean?
questions.explanation: "Looking forward to" = anticipar con gusto. Común en emails y conversación.

questions.question: [B2 - Vocabulary] "The results were inconclusive." This means:
options: The results didn't provide a clear answer
questions.explanation: Inconclusive = no clear or definitive result. Neither confirms nor denies.

questions.question: [C1 - Grammar] Not only ___ she speak English, but she also speaks Mandarin. (does/did)
questions.explanation: Inverted structure: Not only + auxiliary + subject. Present tense = does.

questions.question: [B2 - Critical Thinking] "According to a study funded by the sugar industry, sugar is not harmful." The best critical response is:
options: "The funding source creates a potential conflict of interest that may bias the results"
questions.explanation: Funding source = potential bias. Critical thinking identifies conflicts of interest in evidence.

title: Prueba de Producción por Niveles
description: Construye oraciones de complejidad creciente: A1 → B2.

sentences.prompt: [A1] Make a simple sentence about your routine
sentences.correct: I go to school every day.
Yesterday I went to the park with my friends.
If it rains tomorrow, I will stay home.
She has lived in London for five years.

sentences.prompt: [B2] Build a complex sentence with a subordinate clause
sentences.correct: Although the evidence was inconclusive, the jury reached a verdict.

sentences.prompt: [B2] Build a sentence using the passive voice
sentences.correct: The new policy was implemented by the management team.

title: Vocabulario por Niveles CEFR
description: Empareja cada palabra con su definición y nivel correspondiente.

title: Autoevaluación Metacognitiva
description: Evalúa si conoces cada estructura ANTES de voltear. ¿Acertaste tu autoevaluación?

cards.back: I have been working here for 5 years. — Acción que empezó en el pasado y continúa ahora. Forma: have/has + been + -ing.
cards.example: She has been studying English since 2020.

cards.back: If I had more money, I would travel the world. — Situación hipotética presente/futura. If + past simple, would + infinitive.
cards.example: If I were you, I would accept the offer.

cards.back: If I had studied harder, I would have passed. — Situación hipotética pasada (arrepentimiento). If + past perfect, would have + past participle.
cards.example: If she had left earlier, she would have caught the train.

cards.back: The report was written by the manager. — El foco está en la acción/objeto, no en quién la hizo. Forma: be + past participle.
He must be tired (seguro). He might be tired (posible). He can't be tired (imposible). — Modales para deducir.
cards.example: She must have forgotten — her phone is still here.

cards.back: She said (that) she was going to leave. — Reportar lo que alguien dijo. Los tiempos retroceden un paso.
cards.example: He told me he had already finished the project.

cards.back: The man who lives next door is a doctor. — Cláusulas con who/which/that/where/whose que dan información extra.
cards.example: The book which I read last week was fascinating.

cards.back: Look up (buscar), give up (rendirse), turn down (rechazar), figure out (resolver). — Verbos + partícula que cambian significado.
cards.example: I need to figure out how to solve this problem.

cards.back: If I were you... / I suggest that he be... — Modo para situaciones hipotéticas o recomendaciones formales.
cards.example: If I were the president, I would invest more in education.

cards.back: Not only does she speak English, but she also speaks French. — Invertir sujeto-verbo para énfasis. Nivel C1.
cards.example: Never have I seen such a beautiful sunset.

title: Mapa de Calor de Habilidades
description: Explora tu panel de resultados: cada área muestra tu nivel estimado.

items.label: Reading — Tu nivel de comprensión lectora
Writing — Tu nivel de gramática y escritura
Speaking — Tu nivel de pronunciación y fluidez
Listening — Tu nivel de comprensión auditiva
Critical — Tu nivel de pensamiento crítico
